# Satya Nadella’s Post

[!\[\]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Microsoft•12M followers

2h

- [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_our-fairwater-datacenter-in-wisconsin-is-activity-7450533900038184960-YPil&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Our Fairwater datacenter in Wisconsin is going live, ahead of schedule.

As the world’s most powerful AI datacenter, it will bring together hundreds of thousands of GB200s into a single seamless cluster.

Congrats to all the teams who made this possible!

[!\[\]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Satya Nadella is an Influencer

7mo

If intelligence is the log of compute… it starts with a lot of compute! And that’s why we’re scaling our GPU fleet faster than anyone else.

Just last year, we added over 2 gigawatts of new capacity – roughly the output of 2 nuclear power plants.

And today we’re going further, announcing the world's most powerful AI datacenter, located in southeastern Wisconsin.

Fairwater is a seamless cluster of hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GB200s, connected by enough fiber to circle the Earth 4.5 times.

It will deliver 10x the performance of the world’s fastest supercomputer today, enabling AI training and inference workloads at a level never before seen.

For AI training workloads, you need compute at exponential scale. That’s why we designed the datacenter, GPU fleet, and network together as one integrated system. This ensures a single job can run from day 1 at exponential scale across thousands of GPUs.

Fairwater uses a liquid-cooled closed-loop system for cooling GPUs that requires zero water for operations after construction.

And we’re matching all of the energy that is consumed with renewable sources.

And of course, it is just one of several similar sites we’re lighting up across our 70+ regions. We have multiple identical Fairwater datacenters under construction in other locations across the US, in addition to our AI infrastructure already deployed in over 100 datacenters around the world, powering model training, test-time compute, RL tuning, and real-time inference at global scale.

Too often during times like this, people go with the current and only later wonder, how did we get here?

With Fairwater, we're charting a new path: doing the hard engineering work, bringing compute, network, and storage into one highly scaled cluster, and designing closed-loop energy systems to meet real-world computing needs.

And partnering with local communities to ensure it's thoughtfully done in a way that is sustainable, creates new jobs, and expands opportunity.

We are thrilled to see this take hold in Wisconsin, and we are just getting started.

Learn more about Fairwater here: [https://lnkd.in/gpdni9gt](https://lnkd.in/gpdni9gt?trk=public_post_reshare-text)

The cost of this hyperfocus on LLM scaling has been compute scarcity in datacenters used by loyal Azure customers, and software and platform experiences degraded by ham-fisted AI integrations that nobody asked for (in part to justify the expense of all this GPU hoarding).I guess time will tell if it was worth it.

This sets a new standard.

Infrastructure often hides behind glossy claims.

Remember this: 📌 Scale without integration is just noise.

I believe alignment beats speed. Especially when complexity spikes.

Three pillars to anchor breakthrough work:
1. Engineer systems holistically, not piecemeal.
2. Prioritize sustainability alongside performance.
3. Embed community impact into design decisions.

True innovation blends power with purpose. Raw capacity alone won't move the needle. Smart architecture drives lasting value.

This is a defining moment for AI infrastructure.

Scale is becoming the strategy.

What’s happening in Wisconsin with this Fairwater deployment signals a new era:

→ Hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GB200 units in one cluster → Massive parallel compute designed for next-gen models → Infrastructure built not for today’s AI—but for what’s coming next

Because the reality is:

→ Model capability is now gated by compute → Breakthroughs are increasingly infrastructure-driven → Speed at scale = competitive advantage

This isn’t just a datacenter. It’s an AI factory. ⚡

And the implication is clear:

→ Faster training cycles → More powerful multimodal systems → Entire industries accelerated

The companies building this layer won’t just support AI— they’ll define its limits.

Whoever controls compute… shapes the future.
